texto: If your confidence is based purely on the way you look, you’re setting yourself up for a) years of fretting about how to appear perfect, and b) anxiety / despair as the wrinkles inevitably set in. (Not to mention the fact that the world is FULL of pretty girls – you’re going to have to dig a little bit deeper if you want to stand out in the crowd.) Base your self-belief on what’s in your heart and mind; you’ll never lose your inner beauty.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Rosie Blythe sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “If your confidence is based purely on the way you look, you’re setting yourself up for a) years o…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a autoestima, anxiety, confidence, útil em momentos de confidence e self-esteem.

frase_original: If your confidence is based purely on the way you look, you’re setting yourself up for a) years of fretting about how to appear perfect, and b) anxiety / despair as the wrinkles inevitably set in. (Not to mention the fact that the world is FULL of pretty girls – you’re going to have to dig a little bit deeper if you want to stand out in the crowd.) Base your self-belief on what’s in your heart and mind; you’ll never lose your inner beauty.

texto: If your attached friends are bugging you about being single (favourite accusation: “You’re too picky!”) turn it around on them. “Do you know any gorgeous single men I could meet?” (This could backfire if they’re keen to set you up and their idea of “gorgeous” is vastly different from yours. And it will be.) Alternatively, be super-sweet and tell them you’re waiting for your ideal partner “just like you did”. (It won’t work if you snigger at this point.)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rosie Blythe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amizade. O trecho “If your attached friends are bugging you about being single (favourite accusation: “You’re too pi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, dating, relationships pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e trabalho.

texto: Lovely as it would be to live off a trust fund and have nothing more taxing to think about than where your next hot stone massage was coming from, it's essential for all human beings to have a purpose. There's no honour in boasting “I don't work, dahling...” If you're not stretching yourself, discovering where your gifts lie, expressing your creativity, or enriching the lives of others, what are you for?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Rosie Blythe que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Lovely as it would be to live off a trust fund and have nothing more taxing to think about than w…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre trabalho, career, finances, com foco em trabalho e finances.

texto: A Princess has beautiful manners no matter who she’s dealing with. She would never stoop to being brusque when giving a burger order, or shouting at the barista who forgot her syrup shot. When we behave like true Princesses, people enjoy serving us, because they’re more likely to get a sympathetic smile instead of a complaint about the long wait.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rosie Blythe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “A Princess has beautiful manners no matter who she’s dealing with. She would never stoop to being…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, kindness, princesses pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de princesses.

texto: As long as you wear clothes you love, which suit your body and your personality, it won’t matter if you’re wearing a dress that was in vogue five years ago; you’ll still look amazing. Also, somebody has to START new trends, and that somebody could be you.

texto: If you eat healthily most of the time you can afford to indulge yourself occasionally, but if you eat nothing but junk, you’ll end up not being able to run for a bus without huffing like a steam train. (The only drawback to being super-fit is that centring your life around your quinoa intake and yoga classes often results in an overdose of smugness which may cause your social circle to shrink.)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rosie Blythe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “If you eat healthily most of the time you can afford to indulge yourself occasionally, but if you…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, diet, exercise pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e termino.
